overview: This R/G Modern deck accelerates mana to deploy massive Eldrazi threats before opponents can stabilize. It leverages Eldrazi Temple for card draw, Kozilek's Command for flexible impact, and Sowing Mycospawn for uncounterable value generation. The goal is to out-resource and out-value opponents through relentless board presence and card advantage.
win_conditions: Casting Kozilek's Command to deal direct damage or draw cards
Deploying large Eldrazi threats to overwhelm opponents
Using Sowing Mycospawn to generate uncounterable card advantage

keep_criteria: Hands with two or more lands and a ramp spell
Hands containing Eldrazi Temple or early threats
Hands with Kozilek's Command and sufficient mana sources
Keep hands with 2-3 lands plus at least one ramp spell (Rampant Growth, Cultivate, Farseek, Kodama's Reach) or a turn-1 artifact (Sol Ring, Arcane Signet).
Keep Kozilek's Command if paired with 2+ lands.
mulligan_criteria: Hands with zero or one land
Hands lacking ramp or early impact
Hands with only late-game threats and no acceleration
Mulligan to 5+ lands, pure ramp without lands, or hands lacking early interaction when facing Boros Energy or Affinity.

early_game: Play lands and ramp spells to accelerate mana production
Deploy early threats to apply pressure and protect ramp
Hold up mana for potential interaction if needed
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Sol Ring/Arcane Signet, or cast a ramp spell.
Turn 2: Cast a second ramp spell or Eldrazi Temple
Turn 3: Cast your first Eldrazi threat (Reality Smasher, Kozilek, Butcher of Truth) or Kozilek's Command.
Prioritize untapping lands for ramp over casting threats if you're behind on board or facing aggro.
mid_game: Cast Eldrazi Temple to draw cards and stabilize the board
Use Kozilek's Command to remove threats, draw cards, or deal damage
Begin deploying larger Eldrazi creatures to control the board
Treat mana as a deployment tool, not just a casting cost.
Hold priority to cast Kozilek's Command in response to opponent's removal or sweepers.
Avoid over-committing to hand if you lack answers
prioritize card advantage via Kozilek's Command draw mode or Eldrazi draw engines to maintain pressure.
Commit threats when you have enough mana to answer a board wipe or when the opponent is low on interaction.
Hold back if you suspect a sweeper or if you're facing aggro and need to stabilize.
late_game: Flood the board with multiple Eldrazi threats
Use Sowing Mycospawn to generate infinite card advantage
Close out the game with overwhelming board presence and direct damage
Emrakul, the Promised End is the primary win condition, providing evasion, extra turns, and card advantage.
Kozilek, Butcher of Truth and Ulamog, the Ceaseless Hunger provide secondary win paths through card draw and board wipes.
Go all-in when you have enough mana to cast two threats or when the opponent has exhausted their interaction.
Commit fully if you can answer a potential board wipe or if you're ahead on board and can close the game in 1-2 turns.

key_cards.name: Eldrazi Temple
key_cards.role: Land that draws a card when tapped for colorless mana
key_cards.notes: Tap for colorless mana to draw a card, enabling card advantage while accelerating Eldrazi casting

key_cards.name: Kozilek's Command
key_cards.role: Sorcery that deals damage, draws cards, or creates tokens
key_cards.notes: Use to remove key threats, generate card advantage, or close out games with direct damage

key_cards.name: Sowing Mycospawn
key_cards.role: Creature that creates tokens and draws cards when it dies
key_cards.notes: Deploy to generate uncounterable card advantage and board presence, then sacrifice for value

sequencing_tips: Prioritize playing Eldrazi Temple before casting large threats to maintain card flow
Hold Kozilek's Command for key removal or card draw windows rather than early damage
Sequence Sowing Mycospawn after establishing board presence to maximize token generation and card draw
Always ensure sufficient mana sources before tapping Eldrazi Temple to avoid stalling
Common mistake: Over-ramping into a board wipe without answers.
Always hold priority to cast Kozilek's Command in response to sweepers.
Eldrazi are colorless, so they bypass color-specific hate but are vulnerable to artifact/enchantment hate.
Sowing Mycospawn is a niche mana source
prioritize it only if you're severely mana-flooded.

matchups.advice: Prioritize early threats and use Kozilek's Command for removal or direct damage to survive the early game.
Play around counterspells by holding up mana and use Sowing Mycospawn to generate uncounterable value.
Race with early threats and use Eldrazi Temple to out-value their resource engine.
Use Kozilek's Command (discard) to remove counterspells from hand. Cast threats in response to counterspells to force them to spend resources. Deploy multiple threats to overwhelm counter suites.
Out-value them by prioritizing Kozilek's Command to disrupt their ramp spells or key permanents. Race if they're ahead, or use discard to slow their game plan.

Value is king. Use Kozilek's Command (discard) to remove their threats or ramp. Track their mana development and commit threats when they're tapped out. Prioritize card advantage engines to out-value them.

common_threats: Watch for board wipes (e.g., Wrath of God, Supreme Verdict), targeted removal, and counterspells.
Note when opponents tap out for sweepers or discard effects.
Track graveyard hate if they're trying to disrupt your ramp or value engines.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Based on signature card functionalities and ramp archetype goals.
